# MCP Conformance Tests - Security
# Tests for authentication, authorization, and security validation

# ============================
# HTTP AUTHORIZATION TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 1: Basic Authentication
# Server requiring HTTP Basic Auth should reject unauthenticated requests
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8770 --auth=basic --username=test_user --password=test_password
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8770 with basic auth"

# Request without authentication should fail
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8770 --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 401 Unauthorized"

# Request with authentication should succeed
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8770 --basic-auth=test_user:test_password
stdout '"result":'

# TEST 2: Bearer Token Authentication
# Server requiring bearer token should reject unauthenticated requests
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8771 --auth=bearer --token=test_token
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8771 with bearer auth"

# Request without authentication should fail
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8771 --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 401 Unauthorized"

# Request with wrong token should fail
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8771 --header="Authorization: Bearer wrong_token" --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 401 Unauthorized"

# Request with correct token should succeed
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8771 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'

# TEST 3: Origin Validation
# Server should validate Origin header to prevent CSRF/DNS rebinding attacks
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8772 --validate-origin=example.com
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8772 with origin validation"

# Request without Origin header should fail
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8772 --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 403 Forbidden"
stdout "Invalid Origin"

# Request with incorrect Origin should fail
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8772 --header="Origin: http://malicious-site.com" --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 403 Forbidden"
stdout "Invalid Origin"

# Request with correct Origin should succeed
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":8,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8772 --header="Origin: http://example.com"
stdout '"result":'

# ============================
# AUTHORIZATION SCOPE TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 4: Resource Access Control
# Server should enforce access controls based on resource permissions
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8773 --capabilities=resources --auth=bearer --token=test_token
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8773 with bearer auth"

# Authenticate and initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":9,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8773 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8773 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"

# List resources
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":10,"method":"resources/list"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8773 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"resources":'
stdout '"uri":"resource://public"'
stdout '"uri":"resource://private"'

# Public resource should be accessible
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":11,"method":"resources/get","params":{"uri":"resource://public"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8773 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"resource":'
stdout '"text":"This is a public resource"'

# Private resource should be denied with limited token
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":12,"method":"resources/get","params":{"uri":"resource://private"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8773 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Access denied"'

# With admin token, private resource should be accessible
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":13,"method":"resources/get","params":{"uri":"resource://private"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8773 --header="Authorization: Bearer admin_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"resource":'
stdout '"text":"This is a private resource"'

# TEST 5: Tool Permission Restrictions
# Server should enforce tool execution permissions
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8774 --capabilities=tools --auth=bearer --token=test_token --tools=safe_tool,dangerous_tool
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8774 with bearer auth"

# Authenticate and initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":14,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"

# List available tools
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":15,"method":"tools/list"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"tools":'
stdout '"name":"safe_tool"'
# Dangerous tool should not be listed with regular token
! stdout '"name":"dangerous_tool"'

# Safe tool should be allowed
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":16,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"safe_tool","arguments":{}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"content":'
stdout '"text":"Safe tool executed"'

# Dangerous tool should be denied even if the user knows about it
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":17,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"dangerous_tool","arguments":{}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer test_token"
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Unknown tool: dangerous_tool"'

# With admin token, dangerous tool should be both listed and callable
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":18,"method":"tools/list"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer admin_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"tools":'
stdout '"name":"safe_tool"'
stdout '"name":"dangerous_tool"'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":19,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"dangerous_tool","arguments":{}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8774 --header="Authorization: Bearer admin_token"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"content":'
stdout '"text":"Dangerous tool executed"'

# ============================
# INPUT VALIDATION TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 6: Request Size Limits
# Server should reject requests that exceed size limits
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --max-request-size=1024
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":20,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Normal request should be accepted
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":21,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"This is a normal request"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"This is a normal request"'

# Generate a very large request that exceeds the limit
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":22,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"$VERY_LARGE_PAYLOAD_EXCEEDING_1024_BYTES"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Request size limit exceeded"'

# TEST 7: Tool Argument Validation
# Server should validate tool arguments to prevent injection attacks
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --capabilities=tools --tools=echo_tool,search_tool --strict-validation
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":23,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Valid arguments should be accepted
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":24,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"search_tool","arguments":{"query":"normal search"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"content":'
stdout '"text":"Search results for: normal search"'

# SQL injection attempt should be rejected
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":25,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"search_tool","arguments":{"query":"' OR 1=1; --"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Invalid arguments: Potential SQL injection detected"'

# Command injection attempt should be rejected
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":26,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo_tool","arguments":{"text":"hello $(rm -rf /); world"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Invalid arguments: Potential command injection detected"'

# ============================
# RATE LIMITING TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 8: Request Rate Limiting
# Server should enforce rate limits
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8775 --rate-limit=2 --rate-window=5
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8775 with rate limiting"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":27,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8775
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8775

# First request within limit
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":28,"method":"ping"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8775
stdout '"result":'

# Second request within limit
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":29,"method":"ping"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8775
stdout '"result":'

# Third request exceeds rate limit
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":30,"method":"ping"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8775 --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 429 Too Many Requests"
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Rate limit exceeded"'

# Wait for rate window to reset
sleep 5

# After window resets, request should succeed again
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":31,"method":"ping"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8775
stdout '"result":'

# ============================
# OUTPUT SANITIZATION TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 9: Tool Output Sanitization
# Server should sanitize tool outputs to prevent XSS and other injection attacks
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --capabilities=tools --tools=user_content_tool --sanitize-output
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":32,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"SecurityTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Request with potential XSS in user-generated content
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":33,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"user_content_tool","arguments":{"fetch_user_id":"user_with_xss"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"content":'

# Output should contain sanitized content (HTML entities escaped)
stdout '"text":"'
stdout '&lt;script&gt;alert(\"xss\");&lt;/script&gt;'
! stdout '<script>alert("xss");</script>'
